title: polypodium davallioides
intro: Polypodium davallioides, also known as the Davall's spleenwort or Davall's polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to Japan, China, and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Polypodium davallioides include reducing fever, improving digestion, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
